subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: With help, your child can try out different digital tools to create writing alone or with others.
Your child can use common connecting words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because.'
Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.
Your child reads words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -s.
Your child can use different ways to improve how they talk and share ideas.
Your child asks questions to clear up confusion about topics or stories being discussed.
Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
Your child can use speaking and body language to share and understand information.
canonical_name: Grade 1 Language Arts: Writing and Communication — Foundational Skills and Expression
coherence_reason: The standards cluster around writing, digital tools, speaking, and grammar, but represent two distinct sub-concepts: writing/composition and speaking/listening skills.

subject: Language Arts
canonical_name: Grade 1 Language Arts: Communication — Interpersonal and Conventions
coherence_reason: The cluster includes unrelated topics (interpersonal communication, technology, conventions), and several entries indicate standards that do not begin in grade 1.
